← All charges

Fuchs Elephant Charge 2019

28 September 2019 · 📍 Nankanga Hill

Results Checkpoints Awards Grants 🗺 Map

Conservation Grants

Funds distributed to conservation organisations from the 2019 charge.

Organisation Amount (ZMW) Purpose
Birdwatch Zambia 135,000 Support Conservation Education officer costs to work with schools and communities in IBAs.
Chipembele Wildlife Education Trust 200,000 Covering costs of logistics and materials for on site conservation education program
Conservation Lower Zambezi 200,000 Environmental Education Programme support
Conservation Lower Zambezi 200,000 Environmental Education Programme support
Conservation South Luangwa 135,000 Due to the COVID pandemic CSL has been unable to host the fun run in 2020 and funds have instead shifted to their very important Human Wildlife Conflict Mitigation Program. Funds were largely used to support chilli patrollers who scare away elephants from crop raiding, supporting a weekly conservation radio show, community sensitization using local drama and purchasing equipment for human wildlife conflict mitigation work.
Game Rangers International 135,000 Towards community conservation education, including popular local radio programs
International Crane Foundation 110,000 Support to school Environmental Education, & logistics in the Kafue Flats
Kasanka Trust 135,000 Conservation school programs including lessons on biodiversity, beekeeping, and conservation farming
Musekese Conservation 200,000 Conversion of a landcruiser for a fire fighting vehicle, patrol support
Wildlife & Environmental Conservation Society of Zambia 400,000 Environmental club support, club weekly radio programs, football for conservation, school M&E visits etc
Zambian Carnivore Programme 135,000 Supporting Conservation Biologist Training, Conservation Clubs, de-snaring of carnivores
iLearnabout 49,570 Develop a digital conservation curriculum
Total 1,834,569
Elephant Charge results archive — data updated annually after each event.
